channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: AMCA Program Enters Critical Design-for-Manufacturing Phase as 15,000 Crore FSED Push Moves Beyond Concept Stage
item.description: India’s Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) program has transitioned into its most decisive engineering phase, with the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) driving the Design-for-Manufacturing (DFM) push under the ?15,000 crore Full-Scale Engineering Development (FSED) clearance granted by the Cabinet Committee on Security in March 2024. As of April 2026, the program is no longer operating at the level of conceptual design or preliminary modeling; it is now deep into the granular engineering processes that determine whether a fifth-generation fighter can successfully move from digital architecture to flight-worthy hardware.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO’s Manik Engine Could Power New Medium-Weight Stealth UCAV for Loitering Strike and ISR Roles
item.description: India’s indigenous small turbofan program, centred around the Manik engine, is increasingly being viewed as a key enabler for a new class of medium-weight stealth unmanned combat aerial vehicles (UCAVs). With an estimated thrust output of 4.5 kN, the Manik engine is optimally suited for platforms in the 1.5 to 2.5-ton category, positioning it between the 1-ton SWiFT demonstrator and the much larger 13-ton Ghatak UCAV currently under development.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India–Ukraine Security Cooperation Gains Momentum as Kyiv Offers Combat-Proven Counter-Drone Interceptors
item.description: India and Ukraine are moving toward a formal security cooperation framework, with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy confirming that an arrangement has been reached and documentation is now being finalised. The development follows high-level engagements led by Ukraine’s National Security and Defense Council Secretary Rustem Umerov, signalling a pragmatic expansion of defence ties between New Delhi and Kyiv at a time when counter-drone warfare has become a central operational priority.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Saab Offers Lightweight Integrated Mast (SLIM) to Indian Navy, Bringing Stealth, Sensor Fusion and Top-Deck Integration
item.description: Saab has formally pitched its Saab Lightweight Integrated Mast (SLIM) to the Indian Navy, positioning it as a next-generation solution for sensor integration, reduced radar signature, and optimized top-deck architecture on modern warships. The offering aligns with India’s push toward advanced, network-centric naval platforms where sensor fusion and signature management are as critical as weapons themselves.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BrahMos Crisis: Mass Employee Transfers Hit Missile Production, Indian Navy Deliveries May Face Multi-Year Delays
item.description: India’s flagship supersonic cruise missile programme, BrahMos, is facing a serious internal disruption that could have far-reaching operational consequences for the Indian Navy. According to a report by Navbharat Times, large-scale employee transfers within BrahMos Aerospace have triggered organizational instability, sharply reducing production output and raising concerns over significant delivery delays.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Rafale M with Meteor Gives Indian Navy a Decisive BVR Edge Over North Arabian Sea Battlespace
item.description: The Indian Navy’s planned induction of 26 Rafale M carrier-borne fighters marks a doctrinal leap in maritime air combat, primarily due to the integration of the MBDA Meteor Beyond Visual Range Air-to-Air Missile (BVRAAM). This combination is set to fundamentally reshape the tactical geometry of aerial engagements over the North Arabian Sea, where range, persistence, and networked targeting define combat outcomes far more than traditional dogfighting metrics.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: China Warns India Over Japan Arms Shift: CCP Mouthpiece Flags Strategic Risks in Indo-Pacific Alignment
item.description: China has issued a sharp strategic warning to India following New Delhi’s positive response to Japan’s decision to ease its long-standing restrictions on lethal weapons exports, a move that Beijing views as a destabilizing shift in the regional security architecture. According to a report published by Global Times, Chinese analysts have cautioned India against deepening military cooperation with Japan in a manner that could be perceived as part of a broader effort to counterbalance China in the Indo-Pacific.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL, inaugurates state-of-the-art Satellite Navigation INS System (G3INS) testing facility
item.description: That is a significant milestone for India’s aerospace sector. The Korwa Division has long been a hub for high-precision avionics, and this new facility marks a major step toward self-reliance in high-end navigation technology.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Simulated LOX Module for India’s Indigenous AIP System flagged-off from INOXCVA facility for Factory Acceptance Tests
item.description: INOX India Ltd. (INOXCVA), a leading global player in cryogenic technology solutions, today flagged-off the simulated Liquid Oxygen (LOX) storage module developed for Test & Trial of Energy Module for India’s indigenous Air Independent Propulsion (AIP) system, being spearheaded by Naval Material Research Laboratory (NMRL), Ambernath, a premier laboratory under Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO). The tank, manufactured at INOXCVA’s Kalol Facility, for AIP production agency partner and was flagged off from INOXCVA by Dr Suman Roy Choudhury, Director NMRL and Deepak Acharya, CEO, INOXCVA.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: BDL delivers India’s first Production-Grade Wire Guided Heavy Weight Torpedo to NSTL
item.description: BDL achieved a major milestone in indigenous defence manufacturing with the delivery of India’s first Production-Grade Wire Guided Heavy Weight Torpedo to NSTL at Visakhapatnam.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: J-K: IAF dismisses aircraft crash reports near Kishtwar, terms it false
item.description: The Indian Air Force (IAF) on Friday dismissed reports circulating in sections of media and social media claiming that an IAF aircraft had crashed near Kishtwar in Jammu and Kashmir, calling them “false.” In an official statement, an IAF spokesperson clarified that no such incident has occurred and urged media organisations to exercise caution.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 10 Indians died fighting for Russia in the Ukraine war, Centre tells SC
item.description: Ten Indians have died while fighting alongside Russian forces in the ongoing Russia-Ukraine war, the Centre told the Supreme Court on Friday. A bench of Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M Pancholi said the situation requires “tactful handling” as there may be immense difficulty in recovering mortal remains from the war zone.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Silicon Photonics Process Design Kit and and Universal Programmable Photonic ICs Test Engine developed at IIT Madras
item.description: Shri S. Krishnan, Secretary, Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, launched two Silicon Photonics technology solutions: (a) Silicon Photonics Process Design Kit (PDK) for photonics chip manufacturing, and (b) Universal packaged PPIC (Programmable Photonic Integrated Circuit) Test Engine indigenously developed at MeitY sponsored CoE-CPPICS, IIT Madras [https://cppics.iitm.ac.in/], in presence of Shri Amitesh Sinha, Additional Secretary, MeitY and CEO of India Semiconductor Mission (ISM), Prof Shanti Bhattacharya, HoD DoEE, IIT Madras, Governing Council Members of the centre. The launch took place on Friday, 24th April 2026. This is a major milestone in Silicon Photonics technology sovereignty for India and will serve as a shared national facility for the Indian photonics R&D community.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India-Uzbekistan joint military exercise reinforces military cooperation, improves interoperability
item.description: The seventh edition of India-Uzbekistan joint military exercise ‘Dustlik’ culminated with a final validation exercise and closing ceremony at the Gurumsaray Field Training Area in Namangam, on Friday. According to the Indian Defence Ministry, the exercise reinforced military cooperation and improved interoperability between the Indian Army and the Armed Forces of the Uzbekistan.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Iran Foreign Minister Araghchi To Visit Pakistan, Oman And Russia
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi will be travelling to Pakistan, Oman and Russia starting Friday night, local media reported. Araghchi’s visit is aimed to hold bilateral discussions on current developments in the region as well as the latest situation in the West Asia conflict, Iran’s state-run Islamic Republic News Agency (IRNA) reported.
item.dc:creator: admin
